column1: URGENT: Sudden surge in cholera-like symptoms at the Sector 7 temporary shelter. Over 40 people affected, mostly children. We have zero IV fluids or rehydration salts left. Requesting immediate medical intervention and clean water supplies in New Delhi.

Community kitchen in Mumbai (Dharavi) reports that their main warehouse was flooded. They serve 800 meals a day but have only 12 hours of dry rations remaining. No nearby suppliers can deliver due to road closures.

Monsoon flooding has completely submerged the low-lying areas of Chennai. Approximately 120 families are currently sleeping on the rooftops of the local primary school. No blankets, food, or clean sanitation facilities available.

A major landslide occurred on the highway connecting Bengaluru to the rural outskirts. The main hospital's supply truck is stuck. We need heavy machinery to clear the path and temporary logistics support to airlift critical medicines.

Severe windstorm in Kolkata has ripped the roof off the community education center. 200 textbooks and all digital equipment destroyed. Children are currently without a safe learning space and there are exposed electrical wires.
